title: Fixing GIT Cherry-Picked Commit Hash Validation for Non-ASCII Characters

statement.markdown: Write a function to validate a cherry-picked commit hash in GIT. The hash is valid if it is a string of 40 hexadecimal digits (0-9, a-f, A-F). The function should handle non-ASCII characters and ignore them. The hash is considered invalid if it contains any non-hexadecimal characters or if it is not 40 characters long. The function should return True if the hash is valid and False otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: The function takes a string as input, representing the cherry-picked commit hash.
statement.outputFormat: The function returns a boolean value, indicating whether the hash is valid or not.
statement.notes: The function should ignore non-ASCII characters and only consider hexadecimal digits (0-9, a-f, A-F).

constraints.description: The length of the input string
The number of hexadecimal digits in the input string

examples.explanation: The input is a valid commit hash with 40 hexadecimal digits.
The input is an invalid commit hash because it contains a non-hexadecimal character (!).
The input is an invalid commit hash because it is too long.
The input is an invalid commit hash because it is too short.

hints.text: Use a regular expression to match the hexadecimal digits in the input string.

editorial.title: Fixing GIT Cherry-Picked Commit Hash Validation for Non-ASCII Characters
editorial.intuition: The function should ignore non-ASCII characters and only consider hexadecimal digits (0-9, a-f, A-F).
editorial.bruteForce: Check each character in the input string to see if it is a hexadecimal digit.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a regular expression to match the hexadecimal digits in the input string.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a regular expression to match the hexadecimal digits in the input string and check its length.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function should return True if the hash is valid and False otherwise.
commonMistakes: Not checking the length of the input string
